methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.0B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo, a pre-IPO AI & Software company, is valued at $126B and has raised $16B+. The comparable universe consists of 1 public peer (Palantir) and 8 private peers spanning similar valuation tiers, including Figure AI, Scale AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI.

The peer-median method was applied across 3 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables. This approach yielded a median valuation of $39.0B, with constituent peers ranging from $29.0B (Scale AI) to $400.0B (Palantir). The wide range reflects the heterogeneity of the AI & Software sector at scale; Palantir's public-market premium materially elevates the upper bound, while private-stage peers (Figure AI, Scale AI) cluster more tightly around the median.

Triangulation across available methods supports a valuation range of $33.1B to $44.9B, centered on the peer median of $39.0B. The current $126B valuation substantially exceeds this derived range, suggesting either significant private-round repricing, material undisclosed revenue or profitability inflection, or valuation expectations embedded in prior funding rounds that the comparable set does not yet reflect. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological granularity (one applicable method), the stage heterogeneity within the peer set (public vs. Series C/F+), and the absence of autonomous-systems-specific multiples. Additional revenue or EBITDA-based analysis would strengthen conviction.
